questions.question: What comes next in this letter series? A, C, E, G, ___
questions.explanation: The pattern skips one letter each time: A (skip B) C (skip D) E (skip F) G (skip H) I. These are the odd-positioned letters of the alphabet. Tip: Convert letters to numbers (A=1, B=2...) to spot the pattern more easily! âœ“

questions.question: What comes next in this series? AZ, BY, CX, DW, ___
questions.explanation: The first letter goes forward (A, B, C, D, E) and the second letter goes backward (Z, Y, X, W, V). So the next pair is EV. Tip: Look at the first and second letters separately â€” they might follow different rules. âœ“

questions.question: What comes next in this letter series? B, D, F, H, ___
questions.explanation: The pattern skips one letter each time: B (skip C) D (skip E) F (skip G) H (skip I) J. Tip: Use EJOTY (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) as anchor points for counting. âœ“

questions.question: What comes next in this letter series? Z, X, V, T, ___
questions.explanation: The pattern goes backwards, skipping one letter: Z (skip Y) X (skip W) V (skip U) T (skip S) R. Tip: Check your answer works with ALL the pairs, not just the last two. âœ“

questions.question: What comes next? A, B, D, G, ___
questions.explanation: The gaps increase by 1 each time: A(+1)B(+2)D(+3)G(+4)K. The differences are 1, 2, 3, 4. Tip: If both letters change by the same amount, it's a simple pattern. If different, track each separately. âœ“

questions.question: What comes next in this series? AC, BD, CE, DF, ___
questions.explanation: The first letter advances by 1: A,B,C,D,E. The second letter also advances by 1: C,D,E,F,G. Next is EG. Tip: Watch for letters going in opposite directions â€” one forward, one backward. âœ“
